title: Angular Basic — Paper 4
description: Foundational Angular concepts: components, data binding, directives, component communication, lifecycle hooks, services, modules, template-driven forms, pipes, and CLI.

questions.text: In a @Component decorator, what is the 'styleUrls' property used for?

options.text: To specify an array of paths to the component's CSS or SCSS style files
To define URL patterns for routing
To list style dependencies from node_modules

questions.text: What is the $event object in Angular event binding?

options.text: A custom Angular object that replaces native browser events
An Angular service for managing events
The native DOM event object or the value emitted by a custom EventEmitter
A template variable that holds form validation state

questions.text: Which Angular directive is used to apply a set of CSS classes conditionally to an element, and accepts an object where keys are class names and values are boolean expressions?
What type must an @Output() property be in an Angular component?
Which lifecycle hook is the best place to fetch data from an API when a component loads?
How does Angular's dependency injection system determine which service instance to provide when a component requests one?

options.text: It checks the component's template for service usage
It looks up the injector hierarchy from the component upward until it finds a provider
It always creates a new instance for each component
It reads the service from the browser's local storage

questions.text: In an Angular NgModule, how does FormsModule differ from ReactiveFormsModule?

options.text: FormsModule supports template-driven forms with ngModel; ReactiveFormsModule supports model-driven forms with FormGroup and FormControl
FormsModule is for reactive programming; ReactiveFormsModule is for templates
They are interchangeable and provide the same directives
FormsModule only works for login forms

questions.text: Which template-driven form validator ensures that an input value matches a given regular expression?
Which Angular built-in pipe converts a string to all lowercase letters?
Which Angular CLI command generates a new pipe?
In Angular, can a component's selector be used as an attribute instead of a custom HTML element tag?

options.text: No, selectors can only be element tags
Yes, by wrapping the selector in square brackets: selector: '[mySelector]'
Yes, but only for directives, not components
No, only CSS class selectors are supported

questions.text: If you bind [disabled]="isDisabled" on a button, what kind of binding is this?
In Angular's *ngFor, which local variable provides the zero-based index of the current item?
Which Angular decorator is used to query multiple child elements or components in the view (not just one)?
What is a use case for the ngOnChanges lifecycle hook?

options.text: Reacting to changes in @Input() property values and updating component state accordingly
Cleaning up subscriptions on component destroy
Accessing child view elements after rendering

questions.text: What is the purpose of using @Injectable() without any arguments on a service class?

options.text: It registers the service in the root injector automatically
It marks the class as injectable so that it can receive constructor injection, but does NOT automatically provide it
It prevents the service from being used outside its module

questions.text: What is 'lazy loading' in the context of Angular modules?

options.text: Loading feature modules on demand when their route is accessed rather than at startup
Delaying service initialization until it is first used

questions.text: In template-driven forms, what does the 'minlength' validator do?

options.text: It sets the visual length of the input field
It limits how many characters can be typed
It validates that the input value has at least the specified number of characters
It validates numeric values above a minimum

questions.text: How do you pass a format string to the 'date' pipe in an Angular template?
What does the --standalone flag do when used with ng generate component?

options.text: Creates a component without a CSS file
Creates a component that does not require an NgModule declaration
Creates a component outside the src folder
Generates the component without a spec file

questions.text: What happens to a component's styles when ViewEncapsulation.Emulated is used (the default)?

options.text: Styles are applied globally to all elements
No styles are generated at all
Styles are scoped to the component by Angular adding unique attribute selectors
Styles are placed in a Shadow DOM

questions.text: What is a class binding in Angular, and what is its syntax?

options.text: A binding that applies a CSS class using [class.className]="booleanExpression"
A binding that attaches a TypeScript class to an element
A binding that applies styles using [style.property]="value"
A binding that imports a CSS class from a file

questions.text: In Angular's *ngFor, which exported local variable is true for the last item in the iteration?
What is the purpose of @ContentChild in Angular?

options.text: To get a reference to a component declared in the same module
To query elements that are projected into the component via ng-content
To get a child component reference from the view template
To access the parent component's properties

questions.text: What parameter does the ngOnChanges lifecycle hook receive?

options.text: The previous and current values of all @Input() properties via a SimpleChanges object
A list of all changed DOM elements

questions.text: Which of the following correctly demonstrates using the inject() function inside a component field initializer?
What is the AppModule typically responsible for in a traditional Angular application?

options.text: Bootstrapping the application and declaring the root component and core modules

questions.text: What does the 'valid' property of a form control in a template-driven form indicate?

options.text: The user has typed in the field
All validators for that control have passed
The field has been touched by the user

questions.text: How do you apply the 'number' pipe to format a value with exactly 2 decimal places?
Which file in an Angular 17+ standalone application typically contains the application's providers configuration (previously in AppModule)?
